channel.title: Science - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

item.title: Ancient seas get a new T. rex as massive mosasaur emerges from Texas fossils - Phys.org
item.description:
1. Ancient seas get a new T. rex as massive mosasaur emerges from Texas fossils  Phys.org
  2. ‘T. rex of the sea’: D-FW researchers identify new ancient marine species  Dallas News
  3. North Texas fossils reveal giant predator dubbed ‘T. rex of the sea'  NBC 5 Dallas-Fort Worth
  4. There's a new T. rex from the dinosaur age — and it ruled the seas with a skull-crushing bite  Live Science
  5. Exclusive: There’s a new T. rex in town—and this giant beast ruled the seas  National Geographic

item.description:

  4. SpaceX launches 24 Starlink satellites on Falcon 9 rocket launch from Vandenberg SFB  Spaceflight Now
  5. SpaceX rocket lifts off in early morning launch from the Space Coast  WKMG

item.title: A joint European-Chinese satellite just went up on Vega-C, and the images it returns could change how scientists understand Earth’s magnetic shield - Space Daily
item.description:
1. A joint European-Chinese satellite just went up on Vega-C, and the images it returns could change how scientists understand Earth’s magnetic shield  Space Daily
  2. Smile lifts off on quest to reveal Earth’s invisible shield against the solar wind  European Space Agency
  3. Vega C rocket launches European-Chinese space weather satellite to orbit  Space
  4. WATCH: A Spacecraft Is Launching to Study How Earth Survives Solar Storms  ScienceAlert
  5. ESA-China SMILE mission lifts off to deliver first global images of Earth’s magnetosphere  SpaceNews

item.title: JWST maps the weather on a hot gas giant 700 light-years away - Ars Technica
item.description:
1. JWST maps the weather on a hot gas giant 700 light-years away  Ars Technica
  2. This exoplanet's sky is full of puffy clouds made of vaporized rock—but only on one side  Scientific American
  3. This exoplanet weather forecast by the James Webb Space Telescope calls for sandy skies and a clear (alien) sunset  Space
  4. Astronomers De-Fog Exoplanet Atmospheres with New Cloud-Detecting Method  University of Exeter News
  5. Astronomers get a peek at exoplanet atmospheres with new cloud-detecting method  ASU News

item.title: Neptune's mysterious moon Nereid may be original survivor of Triton's chaotic arrival - Phys.org
item.description:
1. Neptune's mysterious moon Nereid may be original survivor of Triton's chaotic arrival  Phys.org
  2. Astronomers believe Neptunian moon is lone intact survivor of ancient collision  CNN
  3. Where did Neptune's mysterious moon Nereid come from? It may be the only survivor of the planet's violent history  Space
  4. One of Neptune's 16 moons is not like the others, James Webb telescope finds — and it could be key to fully understanding the solar system  Live Science
  5. This Moon of Neptune Might Have Survived a Wrecking-Ball Event  The New York Times

item.title: Scientists May Have Finally Solved Why Humans Are Right-Handed - SciTechDaily
item.description:
1. Scientists May Have Finally Solved Why Humans Are Right-Handed  SciTechDaily
  2. Two Ancient Clues May Finally Explain Why 90% of Us Are Right-Handed  ScienceAlert
  3. Why Is Nearly Every Person Right-Handed—but Not Every Ape and Monkey? New Research Explores the Evolutionary Origins of Human Handedness  Smithsonian Magazine
  4. Why are so many people right-handed? New study sheds some light  USA Today
  5. Scientists may have finally cracked why 90% of humans are right-handed — and the answer has nothing to do with our hands, it has to do with our legs  Space Daily

item.title: A few rocks spotted near a pond in northeastern Thailand in 2016 have just been confirmed as a 27-tonne, 27-metre sauropod — the largest dinosaur ever found in Southeast Asia - Space Daily
item.description:
1. A few rocks spotted near a pond in northeastern Thailand in 2016 have just been confirmed as a 27-tonne, 27-metre sauropod — the largest dinosaur ever found in Southeast Asia  Space Daily
  2. Researchers unearth Southeast Asia's largest dinosaur  NPR
  3. A new species offers a clue to the boom of giant dinosaurs  National Geographic
  4. A Man Spotted Strange-Looking Rocks Near a Pond in Thailand. They Turned Out to Be the Bones of a Massive New Dinosaur Species  Smithsonian Magazine
  5. The first sauropod dinosaur from the Lower Cretaceous Khok Kruat Formation of Thailand enriches the diversity of somphospondylan titanosauriforms in southeast Asia | Scientific Reports  Nature

item.title: Complex animals evolved up to 10 million years earlier than previously thought, fossil discovery shows - Live Science
item.description:
1. Complex animals evolved up to 10 million years earlier than previously thought, fossil discovery shows  Live Science
  2. Discovery of new fossils in Northwest Canada changes view of early animal evolution  Phys.org
  3. Complex animal life may be 10 million years older than scientists thought — and a new fossil site in northwestern Canada suggests it all started in the deep sea, not the shallow waters where most early fossils show up  Space Daily
  4. The Oldest Evidence of Animal Sex Has Been Found, and It’s Mind-Boggling  404 Media
  5. These bizarre fossils represent some of the earliest moving, sexually reproducing life ever discovered  Scientific American

item.title: Scientists just reversed about 80% of aging in elderly mice in a single month — and they did it by boosting one protein - Space Daily
item.description:

  4. SIRT6 protein could protect against age-related breakdown in chromatin, possibly help reverse aging  Phys.org

item.title: Our galaxy may be full of planets identical to Earth at formation that turned into uninhabitable hellscapes — and the one sitting next door to us has been so thoroughly ignored that a leading researcher called it criminally underexplored while we spent decades - Space Daily

item.title: Scientists May Finally Know Why T. Rex Had Such Tiny Arms - ScienceAlert
item.description:
1. Scientists May Finally Know Why T. Rex Had Such Tiny Arms  ScienceAlert
  2. Why meat-eating dinosaurs like T. rex evolved tiny arms  Phys.org
  3. Why were T. rex’s arms so tiny? Paleontologists finally find an answer.  Popular Science
  4. Tyrannosaurus Rex’s tiny arms finally explained by scientists  News.com.au
  5. T. rex didn't evolve tiny arms because its body got bigger — it evolved tiny arms because its jaws got more powerful, according to a new study of 82 meat-eating dinosaur species that found the same pattern repeating across giant predators  Space Daily

item.description:
1. Scientists find a hidden route to the moon that saves fuel  Space
  2. Astronauts Have Been Taking Short Routes To The Moon, But There’s A More Efficient Way  IFLScience
  3. Mathematical method calculates most efficient Earth-moon route yet  Phys.org
  4. A trajectory study suggests spacecraft may not need to meet Earth-moon L1 exactly — and that small mathematical shortcut could matter for the next decade of lunar logistics  Space Daily
  5. Scientists may have found a hidden shortcut to moon that could cut spacecraft fuel costs  The Indian Express

item.title: The Parker Solar Probe is still flying through the Sun's corona at 430,000 miles per hour, fast enough to cross the continental United States in about 20 seconds, because a 4.5-inch carbon-foam shield keeps the spacecraft in the shade - Space Daily
item.description:
1. The Parker Solar Probe is still flying through the Sun's corona at 430,000 miles per hour, fast enough to cross the continental United States in about 20 seconds, because a 4.5-inch carbon-foam shield keeps the spacecraft in the shade  Space Daily
  2. The Parker Solar Probe is now moving at 430,000 miles per hour, fast enough to cross the continental United States in 20 seconds, and it survives skimming the Sun's corona because a four-and-a-half-inch slab of carbon foam stays at room temperature on the  Space Daily
